steps.title: Track the central contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts spoken sermons with printed books. Spoken words last only as far as memory carries them, while a printed book remains available as a record and authority over time.

steps.title: State the permanence idea
steps.content: Choice A reverses the contrast by favoring spoken messages. Choice B introduces confident delivery, which is not discussed. Choice C focuses on authors being forgotten, a side detail. The main idea is that print preserves words better than speech. The answer is D.
